pageText.title: About Ragers and Rampagers, Recovering
pageText.subTitle: RARR is a fellowship of beings who belong nowhere else.

aboutText: RARR is a place for freaks, monsters and humans with monstrous urges or behaviors to find support in a world that seems to have no place for them. It's the island of misfit toys à la Guillermo del Toro.

We find that once we can accept that we are fundamentally different from the average human, we can begin using our very unique set of characteristics for the good of all and make a place for ourselves in this human world, with considerable harmony.

RARR brings together the wisdom of several 12 step fellowships, generalized in the hopes that it will be helpful to those for whom more traditional takes on 12 step are just too traditionally moralistic.
Generalized, debilitating shame is an unfortunate side effect of religiosity, that sometimes gets in the way of spiritual healing for those who need it the most.
RARR also seeks to sneak around denial which is a monster that gets us all occasionally.

faqs.A: While RARR adheres to the principle of anonymity in practice, we avoid addressing it directly as many of our members could not blend into a crowd if their lives depended on it (and it often has!) These members are thus deeply offended by the word, so we don't use it. That said, we avoid talking about other members for the sake of our own spiritual, emotional and physical health.

faqs.Q: Is there a "big book" of some color I need to buy?
faqs.A: RARR has no official book as of yet. We recommend visiting the RARR site, rarr-recovery.org, on a regular basis, and consulting the standard ancient texts.

faqs.Q: How does one do service in RARR?
faqs.A: Spread the word to anyone who you think might benefit and share the link to the official RARR site: rarr-recovery.org

disclaimer: RARR as a fellowship is as real as the vampires, ghouls, and other monsters that make up it's membership. RARR is a proponent of rule 62.
The 10th step inventory tools, however, are very real, and can help you keep a tab on your spiritual condition. We hope you find them helpful.
